use_when: The user asks to evaluate skill or suite artifacts against SkillGuard standards.
A local control record needs evidence, blocker, skipped-check, residual-risk, or claim-boundary review.
do_not_use_when: The task is unrelated to skill or suite artifact governance.
The available materials do not include a target artifact or an explicit scope.

required_workflow.description: Identify target_path, target_type, requested check, and allowed evidence.
required_evidence: target artifact path or explicit missing-information blocker

required_workflow.description: Inspect target materials before making status or closure claims.
required_evidence: file listing, content inspection, parse evidence, or stated unavailable evidence

required_workflow.description: Separate evidence, failures, blockers, skipped checks, residual risk, and claim boundary.
State the supported decision and any remaining limits.

hard_gates.failure_effect: Return a blocker or not-run status rather than a passing claim.

hard_gates.condition: A relevant check was not run.
hard_gates.failure_effect: Record skipped_checks with reason and impact.

hard_gates.condition: A decision or recommendation is made.
hard_gates.failure_effect: Include claim_boundary that limits the decision to inspected evidence.

skipped_checks.reason: This record defines the self-contract and does not execute a future target review.
skipped_checks.impact: Future reviews must gather their own current evidence.

residual_risk: The contract may need revision when implementation checkers and fixture manifests are added.
claim_boundary: This contract defines initial SkillGuard self-maintenance obligations; it does not prove future checks will pass. It does not prove runtime checker execution, fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, or code-contract validation without separate current evidence.
